{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Return-air grille is located in a garage-facing wall, drawing air from the garage into the living space.",
    "Single-wall metal vent connector is installed with only one inch of clearance to combustible wood framing.",
    "Condensate line has an upward trap/rise before dropping, which will cause condensate backup."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the furnace immediately and lock out/tag out the gas supply.",
    "Seal the return-air opening from the garage and relocate the return-air source to a safe, indoor living space.",
    "Replace or reroute the vent connector to maintain the required 6-inch clearance for single-wall pipe, or upgrade to Type B double-wall vent (1-inch clearance).",
    "Re-pipe the condensate line to ensure a continuous downward slope to the drain."
  ],
  "rationale": "Drawing return air from a garage is a critical life-safety hazard due to the risk of pulling carbon monoxide, fuel vapors, and toxic fumes into the home's air distribution system. Additionally, the 1-inch clearance on a single-wall vent connector is a severe fire hazard, and the improper condensate piping will cause system failure or water damage.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1602.2 (Return Air Source Prohibitions)",
    "IRC M1803.3.4 / NFPA 54 (Clearance to Combustibles for Single-Wall Connectors)",
    "IRC Chapter 24 / NFPA 54 (Prohibition of appliances taking return air from garages)"
  ]
}
